feedback_addressed: Added comprehensive consumer health scorecard with credit delinquencies, savings rate, wage growth, and per-pick credit sensitivity
Reframed AMZN thesis with retail-first analysis: standalone retail revenue, 3P vs 1P mix, margin trajectory, and standalone valuation at 0.5x revenue
Added BKNG historical PE context: 15.6x forward vs 31.61 10-year median and 18x-35x pre-COVID range confirming genuine cheapness
Quantified TJX tariff benefit with historical correlation data from 2018-2019 (comps accelerated 4% to 6%) and Great Recession market share gains
Added MELI credit book analysis: $12.5B portfolio, 4.4% NPL rate vs 2.5-3.5% Brazil banking system, NIMAL compression from 23% to 21%
Quantified GLP-1 impact on MCD: -0.4% current dinner sales drag, estimated -50 to -100bps annual comps by 2028
Reconciled sector outlook with picks: changed from Neutral-to-Cautious to Selective Opportunism Within Caution with explicit explanation
Added scenario matrix (recession/stagflation/soft landing/recovery) with price targets for each pick in each scenario
Added position sizing with entry/exit criteria and stop-losses for every pick
Removed BKNG stock split as catalyst per judge feedback
Added CMG management transition risk assessment with Boatwright compensation gap and flat comp sales guidance
Added DHI cycle risk with ROTE compression and forward PE at top of 5-year range
feedback_ignored: AMZN capex ROIC still not explicitly calculated despite $200B capex plan being flagged
BKNG vs ABNB comparative valuation gap analysis beyond brief mention -- the specific drivers of the gap are discussed but a side-by-side table would strengthen this
remaining_flaws: AMZN at 18% allocation is still the largest position despite the retail margins being breakeven -- the concentration risk is acknowledged but not fully mitigated
CMG is included as a BUY despite flat comp guidance, CEO departure, and high valuation -- the bear case seems to outweigh the bull case at current prices
DHI cycle analysis mentions ROTE compression but still rates it BUY; the value trap warning from v1 deserved more weight in the final rating
what_impressed: Consumer health scorecard with D+ savings rate, C credit delinquencies, and D confidence grade provides genuine macro context for stock selection
MELI credit book deep dive with NPL comparison to Brazilian banking system and NIMAL compression analysis is institutional-quality work
TJX historical tariff correlation data spanning 2008-2009 recession, 2018-2019 tariff round, and post-COVID recovery demonstrates pattern recognition
BKNG historical PE context decisively answered the v1 question about whether 15.6x is actually cheap -- it is
GLP-1 impact quantification on MCD with current (-0.4%) and projected (-50 to -100bps by 2028) impact gives investors a clear framework
The NKE SELL recommendation adds genuine contrarian conviction to a portfolio that was previously all-BUY
